title: GloVe: Global Vectors
concept: GloVe learns embeddings by analyzing global word-word co-occurrence statistics across the entire dataset, rather than local window sweeps like Word2Vec.

exercises.prompt: What does the name 'GloVe' stand for in machine learning?
exercises.explain: GloVe stands for Global Vectors, reflecting its use of global statistics from the entire dataset.

cards.title: The Big Picture
cards.body: Word2Vec is like a detective looking at words through a magnifying glass, one sentence at a time. But what if we could step back and look at a giant map of how often words meet across the entire library at once? That's the power of GloVe!

cards.title: What is GloVe?
cards.body: GloVe (Global Vectors) is an AI method that creates word embeddings. It looks at the entire dataset first to count how often words appear near each other, capturing the big picture (global statistics) instead of just scanning one sentence at a time.

cards.title: The Recipe Spreadsheet
cards.body: Imagine you want to know which foods go together. Instead of looking at just one plate at a time (Word2Vec's local window), you look at a giant spreadsheet of every recipe ever written to count how often ingredients appear together. That spreadsheet is GloVe's global table!

cards.title: Counting Ratios
cards.body: If we look at a huge library, 'ice' and 'steam' both co-occur with 'water' very often. But 'ice' co-occurs with 'solid' way more than 'steam' does. GloVe uses these ratios of counts from the whole library to place 'ice', 'steam', and 'solid' in the right spots in vector space.

cards.title: Is a Table Too Slow?
cards.body: People think GloVe is worse than Word2Vec because it uses a giant table of counts. In reality, GloVe is highly efficient because it only needs to look at this pre-computed table during training, rather than scanning billions of raw words over and over.

cards.title: Local vs. Global
cards.body: Let's compare the focus of Word2Vec and GloVe when analyzing text.

left.text: Focuses on local contexts, scanning sentences word-by-word with a sliding window.

right.text: Focuses on global statistics, building a giant table of word counts from the entire text.

exercises.prompt: What is the main difference between GloVe and Word2Vec?

options.text: GloVe uses global co-occurrence statistics; Word2Vec uses local sliding windows.
GloVe only looks at one word at a time; Word2Vec looks at all words.
GloVe ignores the meaning of words completely.
Word2Vec is only used for images, not text.

exercises.explain: Word2Vec slides a small window across text step-by-step (local), while GloVe looks at the aggregated count of word pairings across the whole dataset (global).

exercises.prompt: True or False: GloVe uses the global statistics of word pairs across the entire text dataset to learn embeddings.
exercises.explain: GloVe stands for Global Vectors because it leverages global co-occurrence counts from the entire text.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete how GloVe organizes its global text data:
exercises.sentence: GloVe uses a global ________ matrix to track how often words appear near each other.
exercises.explain: GloVe relies on a pre-built table of co-occurrence counts to learn the relationships between words.

exercises.prompt: Which of these describes how the GloVe algorithm learns word embeddings?

options.text: An AI that builds a table of word pairings across a million books first.
An AI that scans sentences one-by-one, guessing the middle word.
An AI that randomly assigns numbers to words without looking at the text.

exercises.explain: GloVe looks at global statistics of the entire corpus (such as word co-occurrence tables) to generate embeddings.

exercises.prompt: How does GloVe capture global statistics from a large text dataset before training?

options.text: It builds a global word co-occurrence table from the entire text.
It reads one sentence and immediately throws it away.
It randomly guesses word pairs without looking at the text.
It only looks at the first page of a book and ignores the rest.

exercises.explain: GloVe constructs a massive global co-occurrence matrix from the start, counting how often all words appear near each other across the entire dataset, rather than sliding a small window step-by-step like Word2Vec.
